observations.recommendation:
We recommended that the Management undertake the following:
Direct the Municipal Accountant to conduct regular and periodic verification, analysis, and validation of receivables to ensure their accuracy and existence;
For dormant accounts where records and documents are no longer available, conduct investigation as to the causes of the missing documents or to identify the officials and employees responsible; and
Initiate the request for write-off of the dormant accounts and comply with guidelines and procedures on the proper disposition and write-off/derecognition of dormant accounts outlined in COA Circular No. 2023-008.

We recommended that the Municipal Accountant draw the necessary journal entries to remove from the books the PPE accounts that fall under the classification of semi-expendable items, with a net book value of P3,010,296.44, and close the related Accumulated Depreciation to the appropriate accounts.
Moreover, we recommended that the required forms, registries and reports be prepared and maintained by the General Services Officer in order to establish accountability over these items and to safeguard them from the risk of improper use and possible loss or theft.

We recommended that the Management require the following:
The GSO to comply with the appropriate disposal procedures and documentation provided in the Rules and Regulations on Supply and Property Management in the Local Governments and require the accountable persons to file a request for relief from property accountability to the Audit Team; and
The GSO to establish and maintain Property Cards for all assets under their custody, including animals, in accordance with Sections 114 and 124 of the NGAS for LGUs. These records must be regularly updated to reflect acquisitions, disposals and losses, ensuring reconciliation with the Accounting Unit’s ledger.
Moreover, we recommended that the Management require the Municipal Accountant to record in the books the derecognition of the Breeding Stocks account and/or establishing of a receivable account from the accountable persons based on the result of the evaluation of the Audit Team on the filed request in order to properly reflect the effects of the incident in the Statement of Financial Position.

We recommended that the Management fill-up essential, if not all, vacant plantilla positions in the LGU and fully comply with the posting requirements in accordance with Sections 76 and 80 of RA 7160 and Section 4 of CSC Memorandum Circular No. 27.  We also recommended that the HRMO conduct a comprehensive review of the LGU’s manpower requirements in order to ensure that the LGU is run by personnel with the appropriate competency to manage the operations and the delivery of public service of the LGU and reduce the reliance on the services of Job Order workers.

We recommended that the Management immediately prepare the corresponding liquidation documents to be submitted to the source agency, and return the unutilized balances of the fund in accordance with 7.1	COA Circular No. 94-013 dated December 13, 1994, thus, reduce the liability of the LGU.  We also recommended that the Management timely implement the programs or projects funded by the National Government agencies and other LGUs in order to maximize the use of government resources and prompt delivery of the desired socio-economic benefits to its constituents.

We recommended that the Management:
Incorporate in the LDRRMFIP the utilization of the prior years’ unexpended LDRRMF and specify the list of PPAs to be charged against the fund prior to the utilization of the same; and
Ensure that the proposed expenditures are covered with a revised AIP duly approved by the Local Sanggunian and the Local Chief Executive.

We recommended that the Management continue the implementation of the projects funded under the 20% Development Fund and show their commitment in serving the constituents of the Municipality.  Further, to ensure timely implementation and completion of development projects, we strongly suggested that the Management coordinate with the Municipal Planning Development Coordinator and Municipal Engineer to carefully plan and schedule each project's implementation. This will pave the way for a more efficient execution of these initiatives.

We recommended that the Management revert all savings from completed projects to the unappropriated surplus, which shall be available for use for other expenditures or priority development projects of the LGU.

We have recommended that:  

The collecting officers remit their collections daily to the liquidating officer in compliance with Section 89(A) of the LTOM, 2nd Edition' 

The liquidating officer strictly monitor the daily remittance of the collecting officers; 

The Accountable Forms Custodian strictly update the CRAAF with the individual RAAF of the collecting officers to monitor and readily detect any unremitted collections from accountable forms; and 

The Accountable Forms Custodian should not issue additional accountable forms to collecting officers with unremitted collections to avoid accumulation of cash in their custody.
observations.managementAction:
Collections from the Collecting Officers are ensured by the Municipal Treasurer to be fully remitted on an almost daily basis.

Further, no additional accountable forms are given to collecting officers when previous accountable forms are not fully consumed and collections thereof are remitted to the Liquidating Officer

7. In view of the foregoing we recommended the following:

       1. The management to refund  payment  of  overtime  services  of  BAC
          Members who are no longer entitled to overtime pay.

       2. The management to stop the practice of granting  overtime  pay  to
          the BAC Chairman,  BAC  Members,  TWG,  and  BAC  Secretariat  and
          strictly comply with DBM Budget Circular 2004-5A dated October  7,
          2005.

       3. The BAC to comply with all documentary  requirements  relevant  to
          the procurement activities of the Municipality , and to  carefully
          plan its activities  to  minimize  the  possibility  of  rendering
          overtime  services  and  to  save  additional  resources  for  the
          Municipality.

8. In view of the foregoing we recommended the following:


       1. The HRMO to ensure that  application  for  monetization  of  leave
          credits are proper and compliant to Omnibus Rules  on  Leave,  CSC
          Resolution No. 000034, and CSC-DBM Joint Circular No.  2-97  prior
          to forwarding the same for approval of the LCE.

       2. The LCE to initiate the  preparation  of  internal  guidelines  in
          order to properly handle request on monetization of leave credits.


   9. Management Comment:



   10. The HRMO justified that the grant of monetization of sick leave  was
       based on the provisions of Omnibus rule of leave  particularly  that
       of Section 23, which states that: “Monetization of 50%  or  more  of
       vacation/sick leave credits “.Hence the  grant  of  monetization  of
       sick leave to LGU Abulug officials and employee.


   11. Nonetheless, the HRMO  agreed  with  the  audit  recommendation  and
       assured that subsequent application for monetization of vacation and
       leave credits will be compliant with the provisions of  the  Omnibus
       Rule of Leave, CSC Resolution rule of  leave  particularly  that  of
       Section 23, which states that:  “Monetization  of  50%  or  more  of
       vacation/sick leave No. 000034, and CSC DBM Joint Circular No. 2-97.


   12. Auditor’s Rejoinder:



   13. The same provision of Omnibus rule of leave to which the  management
       relied in the grant of monetization of sick leave was  clarified  by
       CSC under Resolution No. 000034 which states:



   14. “As a  general  rule  what  is  allowed  to  be  monetized  are  the
       accumulated vacation leave credits. Section 23, on the  other  hand,
       is an extraordinary measure to relieve an  employee  from  financial
       difficulties that may be allowed  only  for  valid  and  justifiable
       reasons subject to  the  discretion  of  the  agency  head  and  the
       availability of funds. Hence, Section 23 should be construed  as  an
       exception to the general rule as provided under Section 22.



   15. xxx the Commission resolves to rule that sick leave credits  may  be
       monetized if an employee has no available vacation leave credits, in
       accordance with the general rule under Section  23  of  the  Omnibus
       Rules on Leave, that vacation leave credits must be exhausted  first
       before sick leave credits maybe used.”
